title: aethionema saxatile subsp. gracile
intro: Aethionema saxatile subsp. gracile, also known as the fragrant mountain campion, is a plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial herb with fragrant flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions and wounds. Aethionema saxatile subsp. gracile is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and can also be used in poultices or ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
